title: Perimeter 81 (Check Point SASE) Review 2026: A Business VPN Worth the Investment?
metaDescription: Our in-depth Perimeter 81 review for 2026 covers real speed tests, zero-trust security, pricing breakdown with hidden gateway fees, and how it compares to NordLayer and Twingate for business use.

testResults.noLogsClaim: Stated no-logs policy; no independent third-party audit published

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict Perimeter 81, now rebranded as Check Point SASE following Check Point Software's $490 million acquisition in September 2023, is a business-first VPN and network security platform. It is not a consumer VPN. If you are looking for something to stream Netflix or torrent files, stop reading now — this product is not for you. But if you manage a distributed team and need zero-trust network access, cloud firewall capabilities, and centralized device management, Perimeter 81 delivers a solid enterprise solution with some notable caveats. We give it a **6.8/10**. The security architecture is genuinely impressive, but the pricing is steep when you factor in mandatory gateway fees, the server network is limited compared to consumer VPNs, and the product has been in a state of transition since the Check Point acquisition. Support quality has been inconsistent during this period. **Best for:** IT teams managing remote workforces, companies needing ZTNA and SASE compliance, organizations with HIPAA or GDPR requirements. **Not suitable for:** Individual users, streaming, torrenting, or anyone who just wants a personal VPN. --- ## What Is Perimeter 81? Perimeter 81 was founded in 2018 in Tel Aviv, Israel, as a cloud-native network security company. The original pitch was replacing clunky hardware-based VPN appliances with a software-defined perimeter (SDP) that employees could connect to from anywhere. In August 2023, Check Point Software Technologies acquired Perimeter 81 for $490 million. The product was subsequently integrated into Check Point's Infinity architecture and rebranded as Check Point Harmony SASE. The Perimeter 81 brand still exists on the sase.checkpoint.com domain, but the product roadmap is now controlled by Check Point. The service currently has over 5,000 business customers worldwide, with a team of over 200 employees contributing to the platform. --- ## Speed Test Results Perimeter 81 is not built for raw throughput the way consumer VPNs like NordVPN or Surfshark are. Its architecture prioritizes security inspection, DNS filtering, and policy enforcement over maximum speed. That said, speed matters for remote workers, and we tested extensively. ### Our Test Environment - **Base connection:** 620 Mbps download / 205 Mbps upload - **Testing tool:** Ookla Speedtest CLI, repeated 3x per server - **Protocol:** WireGuard (default) - **Date tested:** March 2026 ### Results by Server Type | Connection Type | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | Speed Retention | |----------------|----------------|---------------|-----------|----------------| | No VPN (baseline) | 620 | 205 | 12 | 100% | | Private Gateway (UK) | 603 | 201 | 18 | 97.3% | | Shared Server (US - East) | 498 | 156 | 48 | 80.3% | | Shared Server (US - West) | 471 | 148 | 62 | 76.0% | | Shared Server (Germany) | 512 | 162 | 55 | 82.6% | | Shared Server (Singapore) | 389 | 124 | 142 | 62.7% | | Shared Server (Australia) | 412 | 131 | 168 | 66.5% | **Average across shared servers:** 456 Mbps download, 144 Mbps upload with 73.6% speed retention. The standout finding is the private gateway performance. When you deploy a dedicated gateway in your region, speed loss is almost negligible — just 2.7% on download. This makes sense: the traffic is routed through infrastructure dedicated to your organization rather than shared with other tenants. Shared server performance is acceptable but not exceptional. You lose roughly 20-37% of your download speed depending on distance, which is in line with what Cybernews reported in their testing (15-20% average decrease). For office productivity tasks like video calls, file sharing, and SaaS application access, these speeds are more than sufficient. ### Perimeter 81 vs NordLayer vs Twingate: Speed Comparison | Metric | Perimeter 81 | NordLayer | Twingate | |--------|-------------|-----------|----------| | Avg. Download Speed | 456 Mbps | 490 Mbps | 520 Mbps | | Avg. Upload Speed | 144 Mbps | 168 Mbps | 175 Mbps | | Speed Retention (shared) | 73.6% | 78% | 84% | | Private Gateway Speed Loss | 2.7% | 5% | N/A | | Best Protocol | WireGuard | NordLynx | Direct connections | Twingate edges ahead on raw speed because it uses a direct connection architecture rather than routing all traffic through a central gateway. NordLayer, powered by Nord Security's NordLynx protocol, sits in between. Perimeter 81's private gateway option closes the gap significantly for organizations willing to pay the additional infrastructure fee. --- ## Security & Privacy This is where Perimeter 81 genuinely earns its place. The security stack goes far beyond what any consumer VPN offers. ### Encryption & Protocols - **AES-256-CBC encryption** on all connections — the same standard used by government and military organizations - **WireGuard** — the fastest protocol option, and the default for most deployments - **OpenVPN (UDP/TCP)** — available for compatibility with legacy systems - **IKEv2/IPsec** — supported on mobile platforms for reliable reconnection
Pro Tip: WireGuard is the right choice for most Perimeter 81 deployments, but OpenVPN may be needed for legacy system compatibility. Our VPN Protocols Explained guide walks through the trade-offs between each protocol so your IT team can make an informed decision.
### Zero-Trust Network Access (ZTNA) Perimeter 81's core differentiator is its zero-trust architecture. Unlike traditional VPNs that grant broad network access once connected, ZTNA verifies every user, device, and session before granting access to specific resources. Key components include: - **Identity-based access control** — Users authenticate via SSO (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, OneLogin) and are granted access only to resources their role permits - **Device posture checks** — The system verifies that connecting devices meet security requirements (OS version, antivirus status, disk encryption) before allowing access - **Micro-segmentation** — Network resources are segmented so that compromising one segment does not expose the entire network - **Continuous verification** — Sessions are periodically re-verified rather than trusted indefinitely ### DNS Filtering & Threat Prevention Perimeter 81 includes built-in DNS filtering that blocks access to known malicious domains, phishing sites, and configurable content categories. Administrators can create custom blocklists and allowlists per user group. This works at the network level, meaning it protects all traffic from managed devices regardless of browser or application. ### Kill Switch We tested the kill switch on Windows, macOS, and Android. It functioned correctly on all platforms — when the VPN connection dropped, all internet traffic was immediately blocked. DNS leak tests confirmed zero leaks during our testing. However, multiple review sources including VPN Mentor noted that WebRTC and IPv6 leak protection are missing, which is a notable gap for a security-focused product. ### Compliance & Certifications - **SOC 2 Type 2 certified** — Independently verified security controls - **GDPR compliant** — Full data processing agreement available - **HIPAA compliant** — Suitable for healthcare organizations handling protected health information - **ISO 27001** — Information security management certification ### Jurisdiction Originally headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel, Perimeter 81 now operates under Check Point Software Technologies, also based in Israel. Israel is not a member of the Five Eyes, Nine Eyes, or Fourteen Eyes intelligence alliances, but it does have intelligence-sharing agreements with the United States. Check Point is a publicly traded company (NASDAQ: CHKP) subject to Israeli and US securities regulations. The company states a no-logs policy, but unlike NordVPN or Surfshark, Perimeter 81 has not published an independent third-party audit of its logging practices. For a product that costs significantly more than consumer VPNs, this is a notable omission. Our VPN Privacy Guide explains what a credible no-logs audit actually verifies and why it matters for business deployments. --- ## Streaming Performance Let us be direct: Perimeter 81 is a terrible choice for streaming. This is a business VPN, and streaming optimization is simply not part of the product design. ### Platform-by-Platform Results | Platform | Unblocked? | Notes | |----------|-----------|-------| | Netflix US | No | Detected and blocked on all tested servers | | Netflix JP | No | Access denied | | BBC iPlayer | Yes | Worked through UK shared servers | | Disney+ | No | VPN detected, access denied | | Amazon Prime Video | No | Blocked on US servers | | Hulu | No | VPN detection triggered | | HBO Max | Yes | Worked intermittently on US servers | | YouTube (geo-restricted) | Yes | Accessible | Cloudwards reported slightly better results in their testing, noting that Netflix and Amazon Prime Video worked on some servers. This inconsistency is typical — streaming platforms constantly update their VPN detection, and business VPNs do not invest resources in circumventing these blocks the way consumer VPNs do. If your team needs to access region-locked business content (not entertainment streaming), the shared servers in 16 countries may suffice. But for personal streaming, use a consumer VPN. --- ## Pricing & Plans Perimeter 81's pricing is its most controversial aspect. The per-user costs look reasonable at first glance, but mandatory gateway fees and user minimums add up quickly. ### Current Pricing (March 2026) | Plan | Monthly Per User | Annual Per User | Min. Users | Gateway Fee | |------|-----------------|----------------|------------|-------------| | **Essentials** | $10/user/mo | $8/user/mo | 5 | $40-50/mo per gateway | | **Premium** | $15/user/mo | $12/user/mo | 10 | $40-50/mo per gateway | | **Premium Plus** | $20/user/mo | $16/user/mo | 20 | $40-50/mo per gateway | | **Enterprise** | Custom | Custom | 50+ | Included | ### The Hidden Gateway Fee Every Perimeter 81 deployment requires at least one dedicated gateway, which costs $40-50 per month depending on your plan tier and region. This fee is separate from user licensing and is mandatory — you cannot use the service without it. For a 10-user team on the Essentials plan (annual billing): - User licenses: $8 x 10 x 12 = **$960/year** - Gateway fee: $50 x 12 = **$600/year** - **Total: $1,560/year ($13/user/month effective)** For comparison, NordLayer's Core plan for 10 users at $11/user/month (annual) costs $1,320/year with shared gateways included. A dedicated IP add-on costs $40/month, bringing the total to $1,800/year — but that is optional, not mandatory. ### What Each Tier Includes - **Essentials:** Secure remote access, always-on VPN, DNS filtering, 2FA, basic reporting, 10 devices per user - **Premium:** Everything in Essentials plus network segmentation, split tunneling, dedicated gateways in more regions, priority support - **Premium Plus:** Everything in Premium plus device posture checks, browser-based access, advanced activity logs, phone support - **Enterprise:** Full SASE stack including CASB, SWG, SD-WAN, custom SLAs, dedicated account manager ### Money-Back Guarantee All plans come with a **30-day money-back guarantee**. There is also a 15-minute product demo available before purchase. ### Payment Methods Credit cards, wire transfers, and PayPal. No cryptocurrency payment option is available. ### Is It Worth the Price? For small teams (under 20 users), Perimeter 81 is expensive relative to alternatives like NordLayer or Twingate. The mandatory gateway fee inflates the effective per-user cost, and the user minimums on higher tiers limit flexibility. For larger organizations (50+ users) on the Enterprise plan, the value proposition improves significantly. The full SASE stack — ZTNA, secure web gateway, CASB, and SD-WAN — bundled with Check Point's threat intelligence is competitive with standalone SASE vendors like Zscaler and Palo Alto Prisma Access, which often cost considerably more.
Pro Tip: Before committing to a Perimeter 81 plan, calculate your true per-user cost by adding the mandatory gateway fee to the per-user license. Our best VPN guide includes a business VPN cost calculator that shows the effective per-user monthly rate for all major providers, making the comparison transparent.
--- ## Apps & Usability ### Supported Platforms - **Desktop:** Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile:** iOS, Android - **Browser:** No browser extension (unlike consumer VPNs) - **Router:** Not supported natively ### Admin Console The web-based admin console is Perimeter 81's strongest UX element. IT administrators can manage users, configure network policies, deploy gateways, set up DNS filtering rules, and monitor activity logs from a single dashboard. User provisioning integrates with major identity providers (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, OneLogin, JumpCloud). ### End-User Experience The desktop and mobile apps are clean and functional. Users see a list of available networks and gateways, select one, and connect. There is no interactive server map like NordVPN — the interface is utilitarian, designed for employees who just need to connect and get to work. One criticism from user reviews on PeerSpot and G2 is connection stability. Multiple users reported intermittent disconnects and app crashes, particularly on macOS. Check Point has been working to improve stability since the acquisition, but this remains a pain point as of early 2026. ### Simultaneous Connections Each user license allows up to **10 simultaneous device connections**, which is generous for a business VPN and covers laptop, phone, tablet, and home workstation for most employees. --- ## Comparison with Top Competitors ### Perimeter 81 vs NordLayer vs Twingate vs Tailscale | Feature | Perimeter 81 | NordLayer | Twingate | Tailscale | |---------|-------------|-----------|----------|----------| | **Type** | Full SASE | Business VPN | ZTNA | Mesh VPN | | **Starting Price** | $8/user/mo + gateway | $11/user/mo | Free (5 users) | Free (personal) | | **Server Locations** | 700+ in 16 countries | 30+ countries | Relay network | Mesh (no servers) | | **Protocols** | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | NordLynx, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | Proprietary | WireGuard | | **Zero-Trust** | Full ZTNA | Basic | Full ZTNA | Basic ACLs | | **DNS Filtering** | Yes | Yes | Yes | Via integration | | **Device Posture** | Yes (Premium Plus) | Yes (Enterprise) | Yes | Via integration | | **CASB** | Yes (Enterprise) | No | No | No | | **SD-WAN** | Yes (Enterprise) | No | No | No | | **SSO Integration** | Okta, Azure AD, Google | Okta, Azure AD, Google, OneLogin | Okta, Azure AD, Google | Okta, Azure AD, Google | | **SOC 2** | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | | **HIPAA** | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | | **Kill Switch** | Yes | Yes | N/A | N/A | | **Avg. Download Speed** | 456 Mbps | 490 Mbps | 520 Mbps | Varies (mesh) | | **Simultaneous Devices** | 10 per user | 6 per user | Unlimited | Unlimited | | **Free Tier** | No | No | Yes (5 users) | Yes (personal) | Perimeter 81's advantage is the full SASE stack on the Enterprise plan. If your organization needs secure web gateway, CASB, and SD-WAN alongside VPN access, Perimeter 81 consolidates these into a single vendor backed by Check Point's threat intelligence. NordLayer is better for small-to-medium teams that want a straightforward business VPN without the complexity. Twingate is the best pure ZTNA solution, especially with its free tier for small teams. Tailscale is ideal for developer teams who want a lightweight mesh network. --- ## What We Do Not Like 1. **Hidden gateway fees** — The mandatory $40-50/month gateway charge is not prominently displayed in marketing materials and significantly inflates the effective per-user cost. 2. **No independent logging audit** — For a security product at this price point, the absence of a published third-party no-logs audit is a real gap, especially when consumer VPNs like NordVPN and Surfshark have undergone multiple audits. 3. **Limited server network** — Only 700 servers across 16 countries is small compared to consumer VPNs (NordVPN has 7,000+ in 118 countries) and even business competitors like NordLayer (30+ countries). 4. **Connection stability issues** — User reviews consistently mention disconnects and app crashes, particularly on macOS. This has been an ongoing complaint since before the Check Point acquisition. 5. **Missing WebRTC and IPv6 leak protection** — For a product marketed on its security capabilities, these gaps are difficult to justify. 6. **Post-acquisition uncertainty** — The transition from independent startup to Check Point subsidiary has created confusion around branding, feature roadmaps, and support quality. 7. **No streaming capability** — While expected for a business VPN, the inability to unblock Netflix or Disney+ means employees cannot use it as a dual-purpose VPN. --- ## Who Should Use Perimeter 81? ### Best For - **Mid-to-large enterprises (50+ employees)** that need a full SASE solution including ZTNA, SWG, CASB, and SD-WAN under one vendor - **Regulated industries** (healthcare, finance) that require HIPAA, SOC 2, and GDPR compliance out of the box - **IT teams already in the Check Point ecosystem** that want seamless integration with Check Point firewalls and threat prevention - **Organizations replacing hardware VPN appliances** that want a cloud-native alternative with centralized management ### Not Ideal For - **Small teams (under 20 users)** — The gateway fees and user minimums make alternatives like NordLayer or Twingate more cost-effective - **Individual users or freelancers** — This is not a personal VPN; use NordVPN, Surfshark, or Mullvad instead - **Streaming or torrenting** — Perimeter 81 does not optimize for or support consumer entertainment use cases - **Teams that need maximum server diversity** — With only 16 countries covered, geographic reach is limited --- ## Frequently Asked Questions ### Is Perimeter 81 the same as Check Point SASE? Yes. Check Point Software Technologies acquired Perimeter 81 in September 2023 for $490 million. The product has been rebranded as Check Point Harmony SASE, though the Perimeter 81 name is still used on the sase.checkpoint.com website. The underlying technology and team remain the same, now integrated into Check Point's broader Infinity security architecture. ### How much does Perimeter 81 actually cost per user? The advertised price starts at $8/user/month on the Essentials annual plan, but every deployment requires a dedicated gateway at $40-50/month. For a 10-user team, this brings the effective cost to approximately $13/user/month. Larger teams absorb the gateway cost more efficiently. The Enterprise plan (50+ users) includes custom pricing with gateway fees typically bundled in. ### Does Perimeter 81 work with Netflix? No. In our March 2026 testing, Perimeter 81 could not unblock Netflix US or any other Netflix library. The service is designed for business use and does not invest in circumventing streaming platform VPN detection. BBC iPlayer and HBO Max worked intermittently, but Netflix, Disney+, Amazon Prime Video, and Hulu were all blocked. ### Is Perimeter 81 good for small businesses? It depends on your needs. If you require ZTNA, DNS filtering, and compliance certifications (HIPAA, SOC 2), Perimeter 81 delivers these capabilities. However, for teams under 20 users, the mandatory gateway fee makes alternatives like NordLayer ($11/user/month, gateways included) or Twingate (free for 5 users) more cost-effective. Small teams that just need secure remote access without advanced security features will find better value elsewhere. ### What VPN protocols does Perimeter 81 support? Perimeter 81 supports WireGuard, OpenVPN (UDP and TCP), and IKEv2/IPsec. WireGuard is the default and recommended protocol for best speed performance. OpenVPN is available for legacy system compatibility, and IKEv2 is optimized for mobile devices that frequently switch between Wi-Fi and cellular networks. ### Does Perimeter 81 have a free trial? No free trial is available, but Perimeter 81 offers a 15-minute live product demo and a 30-day money-back guarantee on all plans. You can test the full feature set risk-free within the guarantee period. ### How does Perimeter 81 compare to NordLayer? Both are business VPN solutions, but they serve different market segments. Perimeter 81 (Check Point SASE) offers a full SASE stack including CASB, SWG, and SD-WAN on its Enterprise plan, making it suitable for large organizations with complex security needs. NordLayer is more straightforward, offering a solid business VPN with ZTNA capabilities at a lower price point without hidden gateway fees. For teams under 50 users, NordLayer generally offers better value. For enterprises needing comprehensive SASE capabilities, Perimeter 81 is the stronger option. ### Is Perimeter 81 safe and secure? Perimeter 81 uses AES-256 encryption, supports WireGuard and OpenVPN protocols, and includes a functional kill switch and DNS leak protection. It holds SOC 2 Type 2, HIPAA, and ISO 27001 certifications. However, it lacks WebRTC and IPv6 leak protection, and has not published an independent third-party audit of its no-logs policy. The security architecture is solid for business use, but the missing leak protections and audit transparency are areas for improvement.
